examples: Another neotropical plant, M. holtonii, signals bats via a specialized acoustical "mirror" inside its flowers.
In winter, some of these migrating birds live in the southeastern U. s., but most fly further afield in search of neotropical climates.
This web site introduces the BBIS and provides the following links: Neotropical Bat Information System, Birds, Mammals, Fish, Amphibians, Reptiles.
